message:
feat(tests): add adversarial extension corpus covering T1-T8 threat categories

Introduce tests/adversarial_extensions.rs with ~30 tests simulating
real-world attack patterns against the extension runtime sandbox,
systematically covering all 8 threat categories from the threat model:

- T6 Secret Exfiltration (12 tests): env var blocklist enforcement for
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GH_TOKEN, DATABASE_URL;
  suffix pattern matching (_API_KEY, _SECRET, _TOKEN, _PASSWORD);
  case-insensitive bypass prevention; enumeration and mutation/deletion
  blocking; PI_API_KEY suffix-vs-prefix edge case; multi-vector
  credential theft chains

- T2 Command Injection (5 tests): execSync/spawnSync denial, eval-based
  exec bypass, process.exit containment, process.kill blocking, direct
  exec/env hostcall denial

- T1 Filesystem Escape (6 tests): /etc/passwd access, path traversal
  with ../, SSH key theft, AWS credential theft, write outside workspace,
  HTTP require/npm import blocking

- T4 Dynamic Code Execution (5 tests): eval sandbox containment, base64
  payload decode+exec, dynamic URL import, prototype pollution,
  JSON.parse overwrite containment, setInterval lifecycle

- T5 Resource Exhaustion (2 tests): exponential string growth and array
  allocation bombs

- T3 Capability Mismatch (1 test): hostcall capability mismatch bypass

- T7 Supply Chain (2 tests): tool description prompt injection, deceptive
  slash command registration

- T8 Extension Isolation (1 test): global variable realm containment

Each test loads a deliberately malicious JS extension inline, dispatches
agent_start, and asserts the attack is blocked. References real-world
incidents including MoltBot/OpenClaw campaign and CVE-2026-25253.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
